summary: Comprehensive testing of Role & Permission System Overhaul. 28/28 backend tests pass. Frontend sidebar verified for both platform_super_admin and partner_super_admin. One critical bug found: platform_super_admin blocked by require_super_admin on 2 user management endpoints.

critical.issue: platform_super_admin gets 403 'Super admin access required'. Root cause: require_super_admin in backend/core/security.py has super_admin_roles = {"super_admin", "partner_super_admin", "platform_admin"} — missing 'platform_super_admin'. Fix: add 'platform_super_admin' to this set.

action_items: Fix require_super_admin in backend/core/security.py: add 'platform_super_admin' to super_admin_roles set so platform_super_admin can activate/deactivate users and view user logs
Review PUT /admin/users/{user_id} logic that resets role to 'custom' when modules are updated — may be unintended for users with explicit roles like partner_admin
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL BUG: require_super_admin in core/security.py missing 'platform_super_admin' from super_admin_roles. Line 117: super_admin_roles = {"super_admin", "partner_super_admin", "platform_admin"} — platform_super_admin is excluded. Fix: add 'platform_super_admin' to the set.

PUT /admin/users/{user_id} with 'modules' payload unconditionally resets role to 'custom' (users.py line 195). This means after updating modules, the user's original role (e.g., partner_admin) is lost. Should preserve the original role unless explicitly changed.
Users.py line 62: valid_roles for POST /admin/users doesn't include 'custom' but the PUT endpoint can set role='custom'. Inconsistency.
AdminCreateUserRequest in models.py uses flat 'modules: Optional[List[str]]' but some callers may assume nested 'permissions.modules' structure — potential API documentation gap.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all sidebar items verified, permissions flow verified)

test_credentials.restricted_partner_admin: TEST_restricted_admin@example.com / TestPass123! (partner_code: test-partner-co, role: partner_admin, modules: [customers])

seed_data_creation: Updated TEST_restricted_admin@example.com to have role=partner_admin and permissions.modules=['customers'] in test-partner-co tenant. Created TEST_PERM_COUPON (5% off) during testing (409 if already exists from prev test).

features_verified.my_permissions_endpoint: PASS - Returns {modules, access_level, is_super_admin, role} for platform_super_admin
features_verified.my_permissions_values: PASS - is_super_admin=True, access_level=full_access, role=platform_super_admin, all 12 modules
features_verified.partner_super_admin_all_modules: PASS - partner_super_admin accesses orders and customers
features_verified.frontend_platform_super_admin_sidebar: PASS - All 28 sidebar items present: Platform(6) + People(3) + Commerce(5) + Content(2) + Settings(7) + Integrations(5)
features_verified.frontend_partner_super_admin_sidebar: PASS - 25 items, My Billing(4) visible, Platform section hidden
features_verified.permissions_loaded_on_login: PASS - AuthContext.tsx loads /admin/my-permissions after login and stores in permissions state

context_for_next_testing_agent: All Role & Permission System tests pass. Key bug: require_super_admin in core/security.py missing 'platform_super_admin' causing 403 on PATCH /admin/users/{id}/active and GET /admin/users/{id}/logs. Test user TEST_restricted_admin@example.com / TestPass123! / test-partner-co exists with role=partner_admin and modules=['customers']. To reset: run test_iter160 which handles the update flow automatically. Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter160_role_permission_overhaul.py (28 tests).

rca_of_issue.bug: platform_super_admin blocked on user activate/deactivate and user logs endpoints
rca_of_issue.root_cause: core/security.py require_super_admin() has hard-coded set: super_admin_roles = {"super_admin", "partner_super_admin", "platform_admin"}. The 'platform_super_admin' role was added later but not included here. get_tenant_super_admin (core/tenant.py line 166) depends on require_super_admin, which blocks platform_super_admin.
rca_of_issue.fix: In backend/core/security.py, change line 117 to: super_admin_roles = {"super_admin", "partner_super_admin", "platform_admin", "platform_super_admin"}
